creator: destination_wellknown
caption:
How to spend a perfect day in London - especially if it’s your first time in the city and you want to experience some of the main sights that are actually worth visiting ✌️

Things mentioned in this London sightseeing video:

📍I stayed at the Hilton London Bankside, which was great (not sponsored, I paid £220 for a standard room incl. breakfast) 📍The Blackfriar (perhaps London’s most unique pub) 📍St Paul’s Cathedral 📍Leadenhall Market
📍Skygarden or Horizon 22 (make sure you book online ASAP) 📍St Dunstan In The East
📍Tower of London
📍Horseleydown Steps (iconic photo spot for Tower Bridge)
📍HMS Belfast
📍Borough Market 
📍Shakespeares Globe
📍Tate Modern
📍The Founders Arm (nice place for an outdoor pint)

I used to run a London travel agency, nowadays I just share my best tips for free on this app, my travel blog and Youtube-channel ✌️

DM me the word “London” to get my free e-book with my best tips & tricks for the British capital 🇬🇧

#london #thingstodoinlondon #londonlove

transcription.text: How to spend a perfect day in London. Start the day in your hotel's indoor swimming pool and then head over and get lunch at one of the most unique pubs in London. And if you've got £25 burning in your pocket you can get inside St Paul's Cathedral or you can just enjoy the gorgeous building from the outside. Head over to Leadenhall Market and don't bother going up to this free viewing platform. Instead you should pre-book tickets for Sky Garden or Horizon 22. Walk down to St Dunstan in the east before venturing over to the Tower of London and cross Tower Bridge. Join the tourist trail that takes you past HMS Belfast, Borough Market, Shakespeare's Globe and then you can either head into Tate Modern or you can get a pint with a view of St Paul's.
